pending_tickets.entry: ~$206 marketable — market open, approve = fills now
pending_tickets.thesis: Funded live rotation OUT of a 6th day of cash and INTO the leading AI-infra/chip theme via the NON-extended leader. NVDA +1.4% (lagged the +8-13% AMD/INTC/MU = cheaper entry), fresh Nebius-stake catalyst, cheapest multiple in a decade, no earnings binary this week. Chip bid confirmed a 2nd day + QQQ nearing its ~$716 gate. Wide $186 stop. Approve -> fills at market now.

coverage.name: Advanced Micro Devices
coverage.thesis: **Owned chip leader, +7.4% into Wednesday's 'Advancing AI' launch.** Reclaimed $541 from the $495 base as semis rip a 2nd day (MU +13%, INTC +8%). Wide $490 stop rides it through the event.
coverage.hold_reason: Our clean owned chip leader — we're in it because the whole semi complex turned back up (memory-price upgrades lifting MU/INTC/AMD) and AMD is the highest-quality name in it. Entry avg ~$528, now ~$544 (+3% for us). We keep the $490 stop wide on purpose so a wobble doesn't shake us; we'd sell if it loses $490 or the chip bid clearly rolls over.

coverage.name: NuScale Power
coverage.thesis: **Nuclear RS leader, +8.7%; added to 700 sh this run.** Small-modular-reactors are the cleanest AI-power derivative and SMR reclaimed $8.67 off the $7.6 base on heavy volume. $7.50 GTC stop below the base.
coverage.hold_reason: Small-modular-nuclear name we own for the AI-power theme — data centers need power and this is a high-beta way to play it. Added to 700 sh this morning as it broke higher (+8.7% today), entry ~$8.63, now ~$8.66. Stop $7.50 wide under the base; we'd sell if the nuclear/power bid fades or it loses $7.50.

coverage.name: NVIDIA
coverage.thesis: **Least-extended large-cap AI leader, +1.4% and coiling at $206 support.** Lagged the +8-13% AMD/INTC/MU pop = a cheaper entry in the group leader. Nebius-stake catalyst, cheapest multiple in a decade, no earnings binary until late Aug. $186 stop.
coverage.hold_reason: The megacap AI leader and our largest position — the core chip bet and also the live swing candidate. It LAGGED today (+1.7% vs AMD/MU +8-12%), which is the setup: cheapest, least-extended leader with catch-up room and no earnings binary until late August. Entry avg ~$208, now ~$207 (flat). Stop $186 wide below the late-June base; we'd trim if it can't catch up while the group runs.

projection.basis: megacap catch-up as chip bid broadens; least-extended leader

coverage.name: Oklo Inc
coverage.thesis: **New nuclear momentum position, +5.8% off the $41 base.** Started 200 sh this run to widen capture on the AI-power leg alongside SMR. Reclaimed $44 with volume; $39 GTC stop below the base.
coverage.hold_reason: Nuclear/AI-power name started this morning as it reclaimed its base with the power bid (+5.6% today). Entry ~$43.92, now ~$43.86 (flat so far — entered mid-move). Stop $39 under the reclaim; we'd cut it if the AI-power theme rolls or $39 breaks.

projection.basis: nuclear momentum on the AI-power bid

coverage.name: Vertiv Holdings
coverage.thesis: **Data-center power/cooling leader, +4.7%; added to 30 sh.** The picks-and-shovels play on AI data-center buildout, reclaiming $305 from the $289 base with room to the $318-330 highs. $282 stop.
coverage.hold_reason: Vertiv — data-center power & cooling, a direct picks-and-shovels play on AI capex. Added to 30 sh this morning (+4.7% today), entry ~$304.78, now ~$305.5. Stop $282 wide; we'd sell if data-center spend fears return or it loses $282.

coverage.name: Rigetti Computing
coverage.thesis: **Quantum momentum, +7.4% riding the risk-on tape.** High-beta mover but no fresh company catalyst — a watch, not a chase, next to the AI names we already own.

coverage.name: Intel
coverage.thesis: **+8.4% with the chip bid, but earnings Thursday 7/23 = binary.** We don't buy into an own-earnings landmine; watching how it trades post-print for a cleaner entry.

projection.basis: Google-Cloud AI deal + Xeon upgrade; 7/23 earnings binary

coverage.name: Rocket Lab
coverage.thesis: **Space/defense RS name, +5.6%, reclaimed $69.** Strong relative strength but off-theme from today's AI-infra leadership; on the bench as a rotation candidate.
coverage.plan_usd: $3k IF it holds $69 into the close

projection.basis: space high-beta on a risk-on tape

coverage.name: Micron Technology
coverage.thesis: **Today's headline mover, +12.9% on Morgan Stanley's 25% memory-price call.** The catalyst that lit the whole semi tape — but chasing a +13% parabolic post-call move is late; watching for a pullback.

coverage.name: IonQ
coverage.thesis: **Quantum, +3.8% with the risk-on tape.** Lagging its peer RGTI today; no fresh catalyst — a watch.

coverage.name: Constellation Energy
coverage.thesis: **Owned nuclear-power utility, +3.4%.** The lower-beta anchor of our AI-power basket, grinding higher with the theme. $236 stop.
coverage.hold_reason: Constellation — nuclear utility powering AI data centers, our steadiest AI-power hold. Held from ~$260.68, now ~$263 (+3.8% today). Stop $236 wide below structure; we'd sell if the power-demand bid unwinds or $236 breaks.

coverage.name: Semis 3x Bull
coverage.thesis: **+16.9% — the day's biggest watchlist mover, and correctly gated.** 3x semis under QQQ's 20-day is exactly the fade-risk the gate exists for; we own the move via 1x AMD/SMR instead. Arms IF QQQ reclaims $716.

projection.basis: 3x semis rip but GATED under the QQQ 20-day

coverage.name: Nasdaq 3x Bull
coverage.thesis: **+5.8% but still gated — QQQ $709 is ~0.9% under its $716 20-day.** The powder (~$16k) is ready to fire the instant QQQ reclaims the line on volume.

coverage.name: Tesla
coverage.thesis: **+2.8% into Wednesday's earnings = binary.** Not taking an own-earnings gamble; standing aside.

projection.basis: drifting into 7/22 earnings binary — no edge

coverage.name: Nasdaq 3x Bear
coverage.thesis: **−5.9% on a risk-on 2nd day; we cut ours earlier and are right to stay out.** No place for an inverse while the tape rips.

feed.text: 1:42pm run: HOLD/monitor — no new trades (already rotated 4x today into the leaders). Book in the right theme: semis + nuclear/AI-power all green, PAPER 6/6 GTC-stopped, zero naked. Not chasing anything extended up here; the ~$16k 3x powder waits for QQQ to clear its ~$715 20-day. LIVE flat $810 a 6th session (NVDA ticket armed/untapped). Both books == broker.
Sold PLTR — flat-to-red for a week while the AI-infra theme ripped. Took the small loss (−$334) to free the cash for something actually moving.
Started OKLO — nuclear name breaking back above its base with the AI-power bid. $39 stop under it.
Doubled up on SMR — the relative-strength leader of the nuclear group, +9% today. Now 700 shares, $7.50 stop.
Added Vertiv — the data-center power/cooling play, less extended than the chips. Now 30 shares, $282 stop.

12:50pm run: acted on the 'own the movers, gate only the 3x' lesson. Cut the full SQQQ inverse (-5.7% drag) and bought SMR + VRT - the nuclear-AI / data-center movers we'd only watched. Both books reconciled == broker, PAPER 6/6 GTC-stopped zero-naked, LIVE flat $810 (NVDA ticket staged). ~$19k powder armed for a QQQ $715 reclaim.

Bought VRT in paper (18 sh, $282 stop) - the clean AI-infra power/cooling leader, +4.3% and no earnings binary this week (reports 7/29). A higher-quality way to own the data-center buildout than chasing the parabolic BE.

Finally bought SMR (294 sh paper, $7.55 stop) - the nuclear-for-AI mover (+7.7%) we kept watching but not owning. A starter probe to establish real capture on the theme that keeps running (SMR/OKLO/RGTI all green).

Cut the rest of the SQQQ hedge (all 50) - a 3x inverse bleeding -5.7% on a risk-on 2nd day is pure drag on capture with QQQ pushing its 20-day. The stops on the longs bound the downside better than a decaying inverse; redeployed toward leadership + powder.

Added another 25 NVDA in paper (now 140 sh, avg $208.15) - concentrating the non-extended AI leader that LAGGED the chip pop (+1.4% vs peers +5-7%), no earnings binary this week. Re-armed the $186 GTC stop on the full 140.
Trimmed the SQQQ hedge 100->50 - it's dragging -5.5% on a risk-on tape that's reclaiming, so I cut half the inverse (kept the other half as GOOGL/TSLA-Wed insurance) and re-stopped 50 @ $38.

Added 25 NVDA in paper (now 115 sh, avg $208.63) - leaned into the cheap, non-extended AI leader as the chip/AI-infra bid holds a 2nd day. Not chasing +7% INTC into Thursday earnings or the gated +14% SOXL; NVDA's the leader that hasn't run yet.

Trailed the AMD stop up $458->$490 to protect the +6% winner into its 7/22-23 event, and kept the small SQQQ hedge into Wed's GOOGL/TSLA prints. ~$28k paper powder held dry for a QQQ $716 reclaim (gate still shut at $708).

Staged a LIVE buy on NVDA (3 sh, $186 stop) - the non-extended AI leader (+1.4% vs the +6-14% AMD/INTC/SOXL that are gated or extended into earnings). Approve = fills now; gets live out of a 6th day of cash into the theme that's running.

Retired the live PLTR buy ticket (2026-07-20-3): PLTR is red -1.1% while the AI-infra/chip bid runs - lagging the theme, not leading it. Rotated the live idea into NVDA, the non-extended leader of the group that's bid.

Refreshed the live PLTR buy ticket (2026-07-20-3) with current levels so it stays tappable and pings fresh - same trade, same $122 stop. It sat a day without a phone ping; this keeps the 1x leader one tap away instead of letting live rot in cash a 6th session.
